author: Matthew Adam
title: Royce Lewis goes deep again, but the batting average still stings
desc: Royce Lewis went 2-for-3 in Friday's 9-8 win over the Cardinals with a home run, two runs scored and two RBI.
impact: After Friday's win over St. Louis, Lewis is slashing .198/.285/.349 with five home runs, 16 RBI, 14 runs scored, and three stolen bases across 37 games. He has homered two times in his past three games.

author: Matthew Adam
title: Brooks Lee Goes Deep in Wild 9-8 Win Over Cardinals
desc: Brooks Lee went 1-for-4 in a 9-8 win over the Cardinals with a home run, a run scored, and one RBI.
impact: After Friday's win, Lee is batting .239 with 11 home runs, 39 RBI, 30 runs scored and three stolen bases across 67 games. He is hitting for power of late, smacking three homers while hitting just .179 over his past seven games.

author: Matthew Adam
title: Byron Buxton rakes: 3 hits, 2 doubles, HR in 9-8 win
desc: Byron Buxton went 3-for-3 in a 9-8 win over the Cardinals on Friday with a home run, two doubles, three runs scored, one RBI, and one walk.
impact: After Friday's win over St. Louis, Buxton is batting .266 with 21 home runs, 33 RBI, 46 runs scored, and a .383 wOBA across 59 games. He's been on fire of late, hitting .333 with four homers over his past seven games.

author: Matthew Adam
title: Andrew Morris tosses a clean inning in Twins' 9-8 win
desc: Andrew Morris threw nine pitches over one scoreless inning in Friday's 9-8 win over the Cardinals to earn the save.
impact: Morris picked up the save, his second in four chances this season. After the outing, he owns a 5.10 ERA and 1.63 WHIP with a 1-2 record through 21 appearances. The Twins have used a closer committee this season, so Morris is unlikely to see the bulk of the save chances going forward.

author: Matthew Adam
title: Joe Ryan Sharp in Friday start
desc: Joe Ryan allowed three earned runs on six hits and zero walks while striking out eight over six innings in Friday's 9-8 win over the Cardinals.
impact: Ryan didn't issue a walk, but his bullpen was unable to limit the damage. After the no-decision, he now owns a 3.17 ERA and 1.00 WHIP with a 4-3 record through 15 starts. His next start will be against the Rangers on Thursday.

author: Matthew Adam
title: Cade Smith slams door again, save No. 22 in Detroit
desc: Cade Smith picked up the save in Friday's 3-2 win over the Tigers.
impact: Smith struck out one batter in a scoreless outing to earn the save. After Friday's win, Smith now has a 2.59 ERA and 1.02 WHIP with 22 saves and two blown saves across 30 appearances. He has notched 47 strikeouts and issued just five walks.

author: Matthew Adam
title: Tanner Bibee grinds out seven for rare win in 3-2 squeaker
desc: Tanner Bibee allowed two earned runs on two hits and two walks while striking out eight over seven innings in Friday's 3-2 win over the Tigers.
impact: Bibee has now won two straight decisions after starting the season 0-7. After the win, Bibee now has a 3.96 ERA and 1.18 WHIP with a 2-7 record across 15 starts. His next start will be Thursday against the Brewers.

author: Nicholas Rodriguez
title: Liam Hicks blasts one over fence Friday
desc: Liam Hicks went 3-for-4 with a home run, a double, one walk, three runs scored and two RBI in Friday's 8-3 win over the Pittsburgh Pirates.
impact: Hicks has recorded multi-hit performances in two of his last three games and is currently riding a four-game hitting streak. He has on fire in June, slashing .303/.415/.606 across 33 at-bats.

author: Nicholas Rodriguez
title: Sandy Alcantara records 1,000th career strikeout in win Friday
desc: Sandy Alcantara earned the win on Friday against the Pittsburgh Pirates, allowing three runs on five hits and one walk while striking out seven across eight innings.
impact: Alcantara reached a major milestone on Friday, recording the 1,000th strikeout of his career and becoming just the fifth active Dominican-born pitcher to accomplish the feat, joining Luis Castillo, Freddy Peralta, Luis Severino and Framber Valdez. He also notched his third consecutive quality start, with his only blemishes coming on solo home runs by Endy Rodriguez in the fifth inning and Brandon Lowe in the eighth. After struggling to a 7.39 ERA in May, Alcantara has looked much more like himself in June, posting a 2.86 ERA and 0.86 WHIP across his first three starts of the month. He'll look to keep the momentum going and deliver another quality outing in his next scheduled start on Wednesday against the Philadelphia Phillies.

author: Nicholas Rodriguez
title: Shane McClanahan picks up loss Friday
desc: Shane McClanahan allowed four runs on eight hits and one walk while striking out seven across four innings in Friday's 4-3 loss against the Los Angeles Angels.
impact: McClanahan got off to a rough start, allowing two runs in the first inning. He then worked out of a second-inning jam with runners on second and third before running into more trouble in the third, where he surrendered two additional runs. The left-hander bounced back with a clean fourth inning, but his outing ended there. McClanahan has struggled through his first two starts of June, posting an 8.00 ERA and 2.11 WHIP across nine innings. The positive is that he continues to generate strikeouts, recording 13 punchouts over that span. He'll look to get back on track in his next scheduled start on Wednesday against the Los Angeles Dodgers.

author: Nicholas Rodriguez
title: Taylor Walls swipes bag Friday
desc: Taylor Walls went 1-for-3 with a walk and a stolen base in Friday's 4-3 loss against the Los Angeles Angels.
impact: Walls has showcased his speed on the basepaths lately, recording a stolen base in four straight starts. While he has struggled to make an impact with the bat, posting a .263 slugging percentage in June, he owns a solid .348 OBP during that span. He's worth considering in deeper leagues as a short-term option, as his ability to steal bags provides value in the right fantasy formats.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Colt Emerson hits home run Friday
desc: Colt Emerson went 1-for-4 with a home run and two RBI in the Mariners 10-2 win over the Nationals on Friday.
impact: Emerson capped off a five-run second inning with a two-run long ball. He is slashing .232/.312/.551 with five home runs to begin his career.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Dominic Canzone goes yard Friday
desc: Dominic Canzone went 2-for-4 with a home run and three RBI in the Mariners 10-2 win over the Nationals on Friday.
impact: Canzone smoked both a home run and triple in the win. He is hitting .406 (13-for-32) to begin June with three home runs.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Bryce Miller deals eight innings Friday
desc: Bryce Miller allowed two runs on four hits over eight innings with seven strikeouts in a win on Friday against the Nationals.
impact: The lone damage against Miller came via a pair of home runs. He'll carry a stifling 1.54 ERA into his next start against the Orioles.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Griffin Canning gets rocked Friday
desc: Griffin Canning allowed seven runs on six hits over five innings with six strikeouts and five walks in a loss on Friday against the Orioles.
impact: Canning struggled with his command, while also getting hit hard when he was in the zone. He'll look to bounce back against the Cardinals.
